evidence: The term 'all-powerful' strongly resonates with scale.individual (10) and ontological.material (10) due to its focus on an individual's physical power. It also scores highly in epistemological.identification (8) as it clearly categorizes a quality, and epistemological.perception (7) as physical power can be observed. The resonance.essential (7) reflects its core essence as a defining attribute. Functional value (5) arises from the practical utility of physical strength.

The highest scores reflect 'almighty's' core resonance with physical power (ontological.material), individual scope (scale.individual), existential depth (resonance.essential), and transcendent value (value.sacred). Its permanence and predictability align with stable, enduring strength.

The word 'beardown' strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material), individual-scale strength (scale.individual), and sensory perception of power (epistemological.perception). Scores like functional value (8) reflect practical utility, while essential resonance (7) ties to core physical essence. Lower scores align with its non-abstract, straightforward connotation of strength.

The term 'beefed-up' strongly resonates with physical materiality (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and core essence (resonance.essential) of enhanced strength. It reflects transformative change (symmetry.transformative) and practical utility (value_generation.functional). Scores for perception and experience align with physically observable or embodied attributes.

Brawny strongly resonates with physicality (material, perception, identification), individual scale, and essential resonance. Scores reflect its direct sensory manifestation, functional utility, and core connection to physical strength. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or abstract implications.

Bullnecked directly evokes physical strength (ontological.material=10), tied to individual scale (8) and sensory perception (8). It signals a stable trait (temporal.establishment=2) and has functional utility (7). Scores drop where the word's literal physicality doesn't extend to systemic or abstract domains.

The term 'bullocky' (as an adjective for physical strength) strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical power), scale.individual (focused on a single entity), and resonance.essential (core essence of strength). It weakly connects to epistemological.perception/experience (observing or embodying strength) and lawfulness.predictable/value.functional (predictable utility of strength).

Coercive strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (as a dark use of power) and value_generation.ethical (moral implications). It also aligns with ontological.relational (power dynamics) and interconnection.catalytic (triggering behavioral change). Lower scores reflect its direct ties to physical strength (material/relational layers) and its predictable, societal-scale applications.

The word 'compelling' in the context of physical strength strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material=10), individual scale (scale.individual=8), and the essential core of strength (resonance.essential=7). It also connects to sensory perception (epistemological.perception=7) and catalyzing effects (interconnection.catalytic=6). Lower scores reflect its focus on tangible, immediate aspects rather than abstract or systemic qualities.

Equipotent strongly resonates with scale.individual (8) because it describes equal power among individuals. Ontological.material (10) reflects its focus on physical strength. Resonance.essential (7) captures its core essence of power. Value.functional (8) highlights practical utility, while lawfulness.predictable (8) underscores its stable, expected nature.

Fortified strongly resonates with transformation (10) as it implies a fundamental change to physical strength. It aligns with material/structural layers (10/8) and operates at individual/community scales (8/6). Scores reflect its role in predictable strengthening (6) and its essential resonance (8) with core notions of fortification.

The word 'hard' strongly resonates with physical/material essence (ontological.material) and individual scale (scale.individual), as it directly describes a tangible, personal attribute. Perception (epistemological.perception) and functional utility (value_generation.functional) score highly because physical strength is sensory and practically useful. Predictability (lawfulness.predictable) and essential resonance (resonance.essential) reflect its stable, core nature. Lower scores reflect weaker ties to abstract or relational concepts.

Hefty resonates most strongly with ontological.material (10), scale.individual (8), and resonance.essential (8), as it directly describes physical power tied to the body's tangible presence. Scores for perception (7) and functional value (7) reflect its sensory and practical associations. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or abstract implications.

Impotent resonates strongly with symmetry.destructive (8) as the dark counterpart to strength, ontological.material (8) due to physical power's absence, and resonance.essential (7) as core essence of lacking power. Lower scores reflect its perceptible nature (epistemological.perception:5), contingent causes (lawfulness.contingent:4), and individual scale (7).

Industrial-strength resonates most with material robustness (ontological.material), societal-scale utility (scale.society), and essential core strength (resonance.essential). It emphasizes functional value (value_generation.functional) and predictability (lawfulness.predictable) in its reliability.

The word 'influential' in the context of physical strength (PRP.strong) strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) and scale.individual (8), as physical power is inherently tangible and tied to a single entity. It also aligns with interconnection.catalytic (7) due to the capacity to trigger change, and lawfulness.predictable (7) because physical strength reliably impacts outcomes. Scores for resonance.essential (6) and symmetry.generative (4) reflect how strength's influence can deeply shape circumstances and produce tangible effects.

The word 'ironlike' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 10) and the core essence of strength (resonance.essential 7). It directly identifies physical power (epistemological.identification 5) and aligns with predictable, enduring traits (lawfulness.predictable 5, temporal.permanence 2). Lower scores reflect its focus on intrinsic physicality rather than relational or abstract dynamics.

The word 'knock-down' strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material=10), individual agency (scale.individual=8), and sensory perception (epistemological.perception=7). It also aligns with practical utility (value.functional=7) and core essence (resonance.essential=7). Scores drop where connections are indirect or speculative.

Knockout strongly resonates with individual scale and material ontology as it directly describes physical power. Perception is high due to its sensory nature (e.g., seeing someone's strength), and essential resonance captures the core essence of 'having great physical power.' Scores for destructive symmetry and functional value reflect potential excess or utility.

The word 'might' as physical power strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), individual scale, and essential resonance. It is perceived through sensory experience (epistemological.experience) and identification. Functional value comes from practical use. Catalytic interconnection shows how strength can influence others. Lower scores reflect less direct connections beyond core aspects.

Mightiness as physical power strongly resonates with individual-scale, material existence, and sensory perception. Its essential resonance (4) reflects core physicality, while lower scores in transformation/influence indicate it's a stable trait with limited temporal reach. Scores in destructive (1) and transformative (3) acknowledge potential excess but avoid over-scoring given the atom's focus on strength itself.

The word 'mighty' strongly resonates with individual scale (10) and material ontology (10), as it directly describes physical power. Functional value (8) reflects practical utility of strength. Essential resonance (7) captures its core essence. Lower scores reflect peripheral aspects like catalytic influence (3) or predictable manifestation (6).

Multipotent in the context of physical power strongly resonates with individual-scale physicality (scale.individual=10) and material ontology (ontological.material=10). It also reflects essential resonance (resonance.essential=5) as core to strength, and functional value (value_generation.functional=5). Lower scores reflect weak ties to transformational effects (temporal.transformation=2), sensory perception (epistemological.perception=3), and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical=2).

The word 'muscular' strongly resonates with physical presence (ontological.material, epistemological.perception), individual embodiment (scale.individual, epistemological.experience), and core essence (resonance.essential). It also connects to transformation through training (temporal.transformation), predictable development (lawfulness.predictable), and practical utility (value_generation.functional).

The word 'noticeable' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it directly references physical traits, epistemological.perception (9) due to its reliance on sensory awareness, and scale.individual (8) as PRP.strong focuses on personal physical power. Resonance.superficial (5) reflects its surface-level observation, while lawfulness.predictable (7) aligns with the expected manifestations of strength. Lower scores reflect weaker or indirect connections.

The highest scores reflect the physical nature of the power (material 10), its practical utility (functional 7), its core essence (essential 7), independence (independent 5), and reliability (predictable 6). Lower scores address related aspects like sensory perception (perception 5) and lived experience (experience 6).

Potency strongly resonates with physical/material existence (ontological.material), individual scale, and functional utility. It has essential resonance (core essence of strength) and weak ties to identification (naming), semantic meaning, independence, generative potential, and predictable manifestation. Most other slots lack direct connection to the atom's focus on physical power.

The word 'potent' in the context of physical strength strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), individual scale, and sensory perception. Scores peak where physical power's essence and functional utility are emphasized, while abstract or systemic connections remain minimal.

The word 'powerful' in the context of physical strength strongly resonates with being an individual attribute (scale.individual), perceived through the senses (epistemological.perception), rooted in the material body (ontological.material), independent existence (interconnection.independent), essential to the entity's core (resonance.essential), predictable as a stable trait (lawfulness.predictable), and providing functional utility (value_generation.functional).

Powerfulness in the context of PRP.strong strongly resonates with physical (ontological.material), individual-focused (scale.individual), and embodied (epistemological.experience) aspects. High scores reflect its tangible presence, predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable), and practical utility (value.functional). Resonance.essential captures its core essence as physical strength, while symmetry.destructive and symmetry.transformative hint at potential excess or change.

The word 'powerless' strongly resonates with the individual scale (10) and material layer (10), directly opposing PRP.strong's physical power. It deeply connects to the essential resonance (9) of lacking power's core essence and destructive symmetry (8) as a pathology. High scores also reflect its experiential (9) and semantic (8) dimensions, tied to lived understanding and meaning.

Puissant strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material), individual scale, and essential resonance. It is perceived through sensory experience (e.g., muscular strength) and valued for functional utility. Scores reflect its direct, material manifestation and lack of systemic or abstract connections.

The word resonates strongly with physical/material existence (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It also connects to perception (epistemological.perception) and core essence (resonance.essential). Scores are low elsewhere due to the focused nature of physical strength as a property.

The highest scores reflect that 'regnant' (PRP.strong) directly pertains to an individual's physical power (scale.individual), grounded in the material world (ontological.material), operating independently (interconnection.independent), touching the core essence of strength (resonance.essential), and providing functional utility (value_generation.functional). Lower scores in epistemological aspects acknowledge direct observability and experiential nature without deeper structural understanding. Temporal.continuation and lawfulness.predictable suggest persistence and stability over time without implying permanence.

Reigning strongly resonates with physical presence (material:10), individual scale (8), and essential core resonance (7). It implies ongoing persistence (continuation:7) and functional utility (6). The destructive symmetry (2) acknowledges potential excess, while permanence and broader scales (society/ecosystem) are absent.

Reinforced resonates strongly with ontological.material (physical strength), value_generation.functional (practical utility), and resonance.essential (core essence of strength). It also connects moderately with interconnection.independent (self-sufficiency through reinforcement) and lawfulness.predictable (reinforcement leads to expected durability).

Robust resonates most with physical, individual-scale strength (scale.individual=10, ontological.material=10). It aligns with sensory perception (epistemological.perception=7) and embodied experience (epistemological.experience=8). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential=9) and functional utility (value_generation.functional=7) reflect core physical power. Scores for temporal.continuation (3) and symmetry.destructive (2) acknowledge persistence and potential excess, but remain secondary.

The highest scores reflect the physical basis of strength (ontological.material), the deep connection between strength and ruling (interconnection.resonant/resonance.essential), and potential for destructive excess (symmetry.destructive). Functional value (3) underscores practical use of strength in ruling contexts.

The word 'severe' strongly resonates with **resonance.essential** (touching the core of physical power's extremity) and **symmetry.destructive** (as excessive strength can have negative consequences). It also aligns with **epistemological.identification** (naming the intensity) and **ontological.material** (grounded in physicality). Lower scores reflect its temporal persistence, catalytic influence on others, and practical utility.

Sinewy strongly resonates with physical tangibility (ontological.material), core essence (resonance.essential), and individual-scale strength (scale.individual). Lower scores reflect its connection to perception (visual cues), functional utility, and moderate ties to independence and transformative processes. Scores remain low for non-physical or abstract slots.

The word 'stiff' in the context of physical strength (PRP.strong) strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material=10) and functional utility (value_generation.functional=8). It is perceived through direct sensory awareness (epistemological.perception=7) and tied to individual scale (scale.individual=8). Scores for permanence (temporal.permanence=2) and predictability (lawfulness.predictable=6) reflect its enduring, reliable nature. Scores above 0 are grounded in concrete associations with physicality and strength, avoiding vague rationalizations.

The word 'strengthened' strongly resonates with physical/material existence (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and core essence (resonance.essential). It also aligns with functional utility (value_generation.functional) and the transformative process of gaining strength (symmetry.transformative). Scores for perception/experience and predictable outcomes reflect how physical strength is observed, embodied, and developed through training.

Scores highest in physical/material essence (ontological.material), individual scale, sensory perception, and embodied experience. Predictable as a stable attribute, functional value in practical use, and essential resonance as core to the word's meaning. Scores 2 for identification (categorizing strength) and 0 elsewhere due to focus on physical power without broader systemic or abstract implications.

Vehement in the context of physical strength (PRP.strong) strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), individual scale, and functional utility. Its essential resonance (7) reflects core physical power, while catalytic interconnection (4) shows how strength can trigger change. Predictable lawfulness (6) aligns with the reliable effects of physical force.

Virile strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material, 10) and individual scale (10), as it directly describes a personal trait. It is perceptually identifiable (epistemological.identification, 8) and tied to practical utility (functional value, 7). Its essence (resonance.essential, 7) and predictability (lawfulness.predictable, 8) reflect its core role in defining strength. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential depth.

Weapons-grade emphasizes extreme physical potency (ontological.material 5, symmetry.destructive 5) and functional utility (value.functional 5). It resonates with catalyzing effects (interconnection.catalytic 2) and touches on ethical implications (value.ethical 2). Scores remain low elsewhere due to its specificity to material strength and lack of broader existential or relational dynamics.

The term 'well-knit' strongly resonates with physical structure (ontological.material/structural/semantic), sensory perception (epistemological.perception/experience), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). Scores decline for abstract or non-physical connections.

The word 'well-set' strongly resonates with physical presence (ontological.material), visual perception (epistemological.perception), and the core essence of physical strength (resonance.essential). It also aligns with practical utility (value_generation.functional) and the transformative potential of strength (symmetry.transformative). Lower scores reflect its individual focus and lack of broader systemic or abstract connections.
